chief_complaint: A 35-year-old male presented with a 2-week history of headache, fever, and confusion.
history: Meningeal cryptococcosis (MC) is a serious fungal infection that can occur in immunocompetent individuals. The patient was diagnosed with MC and developed central nervous system (CNS) vasculitis with cerebral infarctions. He was treated with steroids.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, pulse 100 bpm. Physical examination revealed neurological deficits.
labs: Imaging studies showed cerebral infarctions and brain lesions. Blood tests revealed elevated inflammatory markers and fungal antigens.
correct_diagnosis: Central nervous system vasculitis in immunocompetent patients with cryptococcal meningitis
differentials: Central nervous system vasculitis in immunocompetent patients with cryptococcal meningitis
correct_ranking: Central nervous system vasculitis in immunocompetent patients with cryptococcal meningitis
